title: Gulf War General Medical Examination - Research-Derived Non-Public DBQ Substitute

public_availability.status: Not available for public use according to VA public DBQ inventory
public_availability.caveat: This is not an official VA DBQ and should not be presented as one. It is a structured research aid built from public VA/eCFR criteria and adjacent public examination concepts.

source.agency: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs / eCFR
source.research_method: Derived from VA public non-available DBQ list, public DBQs with similar exam structure, and 38 CFR Parts 3 and 4 criteria.

purpose: Organize symptoms for Gulf War undiagnosed illness or medically unexplained chronic multisymptom illness claims.

diagnosis_current_condition: What condition, diagnosis, residual, or claimed symptom pattern is currently present?
When did it begin, who diagnosed or treated it, and what medications, devices, procedures, therapy, testing, or hospitalizations are documented?
What current objective findings, records, labs, imaging, audiology, neuropsychological testing, or clinician observations support it?
nexus_service_connection: What in-service event, injury, illness, exposure, captivity, deployment, hazardous duty, blast, acoustic trauma, or aggravation is claimed?
What dates, locations, units, MOS/duties, incidents, STR entries, personnel records, buddy statements, or exposure records support the event?
If secondary or aggravated, what service-connected condition caused or worsened this condition, and what medical evidence supports that relationship?
functional_impact_severity: How does the condition affect work attendance, task performance, concentration, communication, mobility, self-care, sleep, relationships, and daily activities?
What are baseline limitations versus flare-up or episode limitations?
Use truthful examples with frequency, duration, severity, triggers, treatment response, and missed/modified work.

rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.frequency_duration: How often symptoms/episodes occur and how long they last.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.severity_scale: Best, typical, and worst severity; use 0-10 only when clinically sensible, especially pain.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.work_daily_life_impact: Concrete examples such as missed work, accommodations, difficulty hearing speech, memory problems, lifting/sitting/standing limits, ADLs, or relationship effects.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.flare_ups: Triggers, frequency, duration, what worsens, what functional loss occurs, and what helps.

diagnosis_current_condition: Identify Gulf War/Persian Gulf qualifying service facts and dates/locations.
List diagnosed conditions separately from symptoms that remain undiagnosed or unexplained.
Document whether symptoms are chronic, objective, recurrent, or have existed for six months or more, including intermittent episodes.
medical_history_onset_course_treatment: Build deployment and symptom timeline: before deployment, during deployment/exposure, onset, chronicity, medical workup, and current limitations.
nexus_service_connection_prompts: Identify claimed Gulf War basis under undiagnosed illness, MUCMI, diagnosed condition with direct nexus, or other presumptive/exposure rule.
symptoms_signs_findings: Fatigue, skin symptoms, headache, muscle pain, joint pain, neurologic signs, neuropsychological symptoms, respiratory symptoms, sleep disturbance, GI symptoms, cardiovascular symptoms, abnormal weight loss, menstrual disorders, or other unexplained symptoms.
Symptom clusters suggesting MUCMI patterns such as chronic fatigue syndrome, fibromyalgia, functional GI disorders, or unexplained multisystem illness.
Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, remissions, and whether symptoms migrate across body systems.
Medication/treatment response and whether symptoms remain unexplained after workup.
diagnostic_testing_objective_findings: Deployment/location records for Southwest Asia or other qualifying service.
Labs, imaging, specialist evaluations, pulmonary/cardiac/GI/neurologic/rheumatologic/mental health workups as indicated.
Objective indicators such as clinician signs, abnormal tests, non-medical indicators independently verifiable, symptom logs, work records, and lay statements.
frequency_duration_prompts: Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, remissions, and whether symptoms migrate across body systems.
How unpredictable episodes affect reliability and scheduling.
severity_prompts: Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, remissions, and whether symptoms migrate across body systems.
Fatigue-related reduced activity, missed work, post-exertional worsening, sleep problems, pain limits, bathroom access needs, headaches, concentration/memory issues, or respiratory limitations.
Limits on exertion, social activity, family duties, travel, chores, and self-care.
flare_up_prompts: Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, remissions, and whether symptoms migrate across body systems.
How unpredictable episodes affect reliability and scheduling.
functional_impact_prompts: Fatigue-related reduced activity, missed work, post-exertional worsening, sleep problems, pain limits, bathroom access needs, headaches, concentration/memory issues, or respiratory limitations.
How unpredictable episodes affect reliability and scheduling.
Limits on exertion, social activity, family duties, travel, chores, and self-care.

sections.title: SECTION 1 - SOURCE AND AVAILABILITY
items: VA lists this DBQ as not available for public use.
Use this JSON as a preparation aid, not as a completed DBQ.

sections.title: SECTION 2 - DIAGNOSIS / CURRENT CONDITION
items: Identify Gulf War/Persian Gulf qualifying service facts and dates/locations.
List diagnosed conditions separately from symptoms that remain undiagnosed or unexplained.
Document whether symptoms are chronic, objective, recurrent, or have existed for six months or more, including intermittent episodes.

sections.title: SECTION 3 - MEDICAL HISTORY AND NEXUS FACTS
items: Build deployment and symptom timeline: before deployment, during deployment/exposure, onset, chronicity, medical workup, and current limitations.
Identify claimed Gulf War basis under undiagnosed illness, MUCMI, diagnosed condition with direct nexus, or other presumptive/exposure rule.

sections.title: SECTION 4 - SYMPTOMS, SIGNS, AND FINDINGS
items: Fatigue, skin symptoms, headache, muscle pain, joint pain, neurologic signs, neuropsychological symptoms, respiratory symptoms, sleep disturbance, GI symptoms, cardiovascular symptoms, abnormal weight loss, menstrual disorders, or other unexplained symptoms.
Symptom clusters suggesting MUCMI patterns such as chronic fatigue syndrome, fibromyalgia, functional GI disorders, or unexplained multisystem illness.
Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, remissions, and whether symptoms migrate across body systems.
Medication/treatment response and whether symptoms remain unexplained after workup.

sections.title: SECTION 5 - TESTING / OBJECTIVE FINDINGS
items: Deployment/location records for Southwest Asia or other qualifying service.
Labs, imaging, specialist evaluations, pulmonary/cardiac/GI/neurologic/rheumatologic/mental health workups as indicated.
Objective indicators such as clinician signs, abnormal tests, non-medical indicators independently verifiable, symptom logs, work records, and lay statements.

sections.title: SECTION 6 - FUNCTIONAL IMPACT
items: Fatigue-related reduced activity, missed work, post-exertional worsening, sleep problems, pain limits, bathroom access needs, headaches, concentration/memory issues, or respiratory limitations.
How unpredictable episodes affect reliability and scheduling.
Limits on exertion, social activity, family duties, travel, chores, and self-care.

sections.title: SECTION 7 - SPECIAL NOTES
items: If a symptom is diagnosed under a specific condition, route to that specific DBQ JSON when available.

recommended_user_inputs: Current diagnosis or claimed symptom/residual pattern
Exact onset date/timeframe and in-service event/exposure/injury/captivity/deployment/acoustic/blast facts
Treatment history, medications, devices, therapy, surgeries, hospitalizations, specialist care, tests, and records
Baseline symptoms with frequency, duration, severity, and daily functional impact
Flare-ups/episodes/attacks with triggers, duration, severity, and what activities become impossible or limited
Work impact, accommodations, missed work, safety issues, communication limits, memory/concentration issues, ADL limits, and relationship effects
cheat_sheet_use.truthfulness_guardrail: Use only accurate personal facts. Do not invent symptoms, diagnoses, stressors, captivity details, exposures, test results, or medical nexus opinions.

script_templates.truthfulness_guardrail: Fill in only what is true. Skip wording that does not apply. Do not exaggerate, invent symptoms, stressors, exposures, test results, or medical nexus opinions.

default_options.triggers: exertion, heat, dust/fumes, stress, poor sleep, foods, and flare-ups
default_options.limitation: stamina, work reliability, sleep, chores, exercise, and daily planning
default_options.flareCare: rest, medication, pacing, and avoiding triggers
default_options.serviceEvent: [specific service event, injury, exposure, aggravation, or secondary condition]
default_options.workImpact: I need breaks, work slower, avoid certain tasks, or miss time when symptoms flare.
default_options.dailyImpact: I plan activities around symptoms and need extra time, rest, or help with harder tasks.
default_options.mainSymptoms: fatigue, pain, headaches, respiratory symptoms, GI symptoms, skin symptoms, sleep issues, or unexplained symptoms if true
default_options.evidence: service records, treatment records, VA/private records, medications, imaging/labs/testing, profiles, buddy statements, or lay statements if true

sections.title: Medical History / Onset & Nexus
sections.intent: Answer when/where symptoms started, what service event/exposure/aggravation may connect them, and what treatment has been tried.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: current diagnosis or claimed condition, onset, course, treatment history, medications, testing, and current symptoms; in-service event, injury, illness, hazardous exposure, aggravation, or secondary service connection timeline; treatment records, medications, tests, imaging/labs, specialist care, therapy, devices, and supporting evidence
sections.what_to_say_template: My claimed condition is {{currentDiagnosis}}. It first started or became noticeable {{onset}} after {{serviceEvent}}. Since then, the condition has continued with {{mainSymptoms}}. I have tried {{treatment}}, but symptoms still happen {{frequency}}. Evidence that may support the timeline includes {{evidence}}.

default_options.triggers: exertion, heat, dust/fumes, stress, poor sleep, foods, and flare-ups
default_options.limitation: stamina, work reliability, sleep, chores, exercise, and daily planning
default_options.flareCare: rest, medication, pacing, and avoiding triggers
default_options.serviceEvent: [specific service event, injury, exposure, aggravation, or secondary condition]
default_options.workImpact: I need breaks, work slower, avoid certain tasks, or miss time when symptoms flare.
default_options.dailyImpact: I plan activities around symptoms and need extra time, rest, or help with harder tasks.
default_options.mainSymptoms: fatigue, pain, headaches, respiratory symptoms, GI symptoms, skin symptoms, sleep issues, or unexplained symptoms if true
default_options.evidence: service records, treatment records, VA/private records, medications, imaging/labs/testing, profiles, buddy statements, or lay statements if true

sections.title: Symptom Evaluation
sections.intent: Answer DBQ-style symptom questions with exact frequency, duration, severity, triggers, and condition-specific symptoms.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: frequency, duration, severity, flare-ups, treatment, work impact, and daily activity limits
sections.what_to_say_template: My symptoms are {{mainSymptoms}}. They happen {{frequency}}, usually last {{duration}}, and are triggered or worsened by {{triggers}}. On better days they are about {{bestPain}}; on worst days they reach {{worstPain}}. For example, symptoms happen {{frequency}}, last {{duration}}, and worsen with {{triggers}}.

default_options.triggers: exertion, heat, dust/fumes, stress, poor sleep, foods, and flare-ups
default_options.limitation: stamina, work reliability, sleep, chores, exercise, and daily planning
default_options.flareCare: rest, medication, pacing, and avoiding triggers
default_options.serviceEvent: [specific service event, injury, exposure, aggravation, or secondary condition]
default_options.workImpact: I need breaks, work slower, avoid certain tasks, or miss time when symptoms flare.
default_options.dailyImpact: I plan activities around symptoms and need extra time, rest, or help with harder tasks.
default_options.mainSymptoms: fatigue, pain, headaches, respiratory symptoms, GI symptoms, skin symptoms, sleep issues, or unexplained symptoms if true
default_options.evidence: service records, treatment records, VA/private records, medications, imaging/labs/testing, profiles, buddy statements, or lay statements if true

subsections.title: Frequency & Duration
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Document frequency, duration, pattern, triggers, and whether symptoms are constant, intermittent, recurrent, or episodic.
subsections.what_to_say_template: My {{currentDiagnosis}} symptoms are {{frequency}}. They usually last {{duration}}. The symptoms I need documented are {{mainSymptoms}}. They get worse with {{triggers}}.
subsections.example_template: For example, symptoms happen {{frequency}}, last {{duration}}, and worsen with {{triggers}}.

subsections.title: Severity Scale
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Describe severity using measurable words, worst-day impairment, and any DBQ-specific severe-episode language that truthfully applies.
subsections.what_to_say_template: On better days, severity is about {{bestPain}}. On worst days, it reaches {{worstPain}}. Overall, I would describe it as {{severity}} because it interferes with {{limitation}}.
subsections.example_template: On a bad day, symptoms reach {{worstPain}} and I have to stop or reduce {{limitation}} instead of pushing through.

sections.title: Functional Impact / Occupational Assessment
sections.intent: Answer how the disability limits work, daily activities, relationships/social function, reliability, and worst-day functioning.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: frequency, duration, severity, flare-ups, treatment, work impact, and daily activity limits

default_options.triggers: exertion, heat, dust/fumes, stress, poor sleep, foods, and flare-ups
default_options.limitation: stamina, work reliability, sleep, chores, exercise, and daily planning
default_options.flareCare: rest, medication, pacing, and avoiding triggers
default_options.serviceEvent: [specific service event, injury, exposure, aggravation, or secondary condition]

subsections.title: Daily Activities
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Explain work limitations, daily activity limits, missed time, accommodations, safety concerns, and reduced reliability/productivity.
subsections.what_to_say_template: This affects work because {{workImpact}} It affects daily life because {{dailyImpact}} I can usually continue for about {{limitBeforeStopping}} before I need to stop, rest, modify the task, or get help.
subsections.example_template: A real-life example is: symptoms affect reliability, pace, attendance, safety, or task completion, and I need breaks or modifications.

subsections.title: Flare-Ups / Worst Days
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Describe flare-up frequency, triggers, duration, worst-day functional loss, and what helps relieve symptoms.
subsections.what_to_say_template: Flare-ups or worst episodes happen {{flareUps}}. They are triggered by {{triggers}} and can last {{duration}}. During them, I cannot keep up with {{limitation}}, and I use {{flareCare}}.
subsections.example_template: During a flare-up, symptoms worsen to {{worstPain}}, I reduce activity, use {{flareCare}}, and may need help or time to recover.
